_comment: UBA park factors by team for VOS Evaluation
_comment_2: Team keys match config/uba_orgs.json. Atlanta Bandits uses AFLAC Ballpark OOTP data; remaining teams are neutral placeholders until team-specific park factors are entered

Atlanta Bandits._comment: Park factors for Atlanta Bandits (UBA) - VOS Evaluation
Atlanta Bandits._comment_2: AFLAC Ballpark — retractable roof, grass, capacity 45,000 (OOTP park factors)
Atlanta Bandits._comment_3: Each team should have its own park factors file (e.g., park-factors-{team-code}.json)

raw_park_factors._comment: Raw park factors from OOTP - 1.0 = league average

park_profile.description: AFLAC Ballpark (retractable roof, grass) suppresses batting average and home runs slightly while boosting doubles and triples. Favors gap hitters over pure power; modest RHB HR edge.
key_characteristics: Small RHB vs LHB HR split (~2.1% relative)

tool_adjustments._comment: Multipliers applied to tool ratings. 1.0 = no change, >1.0 = more valuable, <1.0 = less valuable
tool_adjustments._comment_2: These are calculated from raw park factors using conversion formulas

defense._comment: Elevated doubles/triples with slightly depressed average — outfield range still matters for gaps
defense._rationale_OFR: Above-average 2B/3B rates reward outfield range on balls in the alleys
defense._rationale_IFR: Slightly below-average average implies marginally fewer balls to infielders

baserunning._comment: Modest doubles/triples environment rewards speed and reads slightly
baserunning._rationale_Speed: Extra-base opportunities slightly above average
baserunning._rationale_Run: Triples rate nudges value of smart baserunning

pitcher_ability._comment: Park characteristics affect which pitcher skills are most valuable
pitcher_ability._rationale_Movement: Some help limiting hard contact in the gaps
pitcher_ability._rationale_Control: Marginally more important with slightly depressed average
pitcher_ability._rationale_HR_Avoid: Mild HR suppression — HR avoidance matters a bit less than in a big HR park

handedness_splits._comment: RHB 0.960 vs LHB 0.980 HR factors — small split
handedness_splits._comment_2: Optional handedness-specific adjustments - only used if use_handedness_splits is true
handedness_splits._rationale: Splits are modest (~2% HR difference) — optional complexity

application_rules._comment: Rules for when and how aggressively to apply these park factors
application_rules._comment_prospects: Apply to all players in Atlanta Bandits organization, including minors
application_rules._comment_major_leaguers: Apply to players at ML level with this team
application_rules._comment_handedness: Disabled by default — RHB/LHB HR gap is small
application_rules._comment_strength: Use 50% strength as conservative starting point. 1.0 = full strength, 0.5 = half strength
application_rules._comment_strength_2: At 0.5 strength: Pow becomes 0.990 instead of 0.980

usage_notes.creating_files_for_other_teams: Copy this team entry and adjust raw_park_factors and team_info sections
usage_notes.tuning_adjustment_strength: Start at 0.5, increase to 0.7-1.0 if you want more aggressive park effects
usage_notes.when_to_enable_handedness_splits: Consider if RHB/LHB HR factors differ by >10% (here ~2%)

Cape Fear Carnivores._comment: Park factors for Cape Fear Carnivores (UBA) - VOS Evaluation
Cape Fear Carnivores._comment_2: PLACEHOLDER: neutral 1.000 raw factors until OOTP park data is supplied
Cape Fear Carnivores._comment_3: Each team should have its own park factors file (e.g., park-factors-{team-code}.json)

raw_park_factors._comment: Raw park factors from OOTP - 1.0 = league average

park_profile.description: Placeholder Park registers as a true neutral venue: batting average, extra-base hits, and home runs (overall and by handedness) all sit at league average until UBA-specific OOTP park data is entered for Cape Fear Carnivores.
key_characteristics: Home runs league average overall and by hand (1.000)
No meaningful handedness asymmetry in the export

tool_adjustments._comment: Multipliers applied to tool ratings. 1.0 = no change, >1.0 = more valuable, <1.0 = less valuable
tool_adjustments._comment_2: All tools 1.0: conversion formulas on 1.000 raw factors yield no adjustment

defense._comment: Neutral park - baseline defensive tool weights

baserunning._rationale_Speed: No extra-base or run-environment tilt in the export
baserunning._rationale_Run: No extra-base or run-environment tilt in the export

pitcher_ability._comment: Neutral park - baseline pitcher tool weights

handedness_splits._comment_2: OOTP export is symmetric by handedness for these factors
handedness_splits._rationale: RHB and LHB HR factors identical at league average

application_rules._comment: Rules for when and how aggressively to apply these park factors
application_rules._comment_prospects: Apply to all players in Cape Fear Carnivores organization, including minors
application_rules._comment_major_leaguers: Apply to players at ML level with this team
application_rules._comment_handedness: Disabled: no RHB/LHB HR difference in export
application_rules._comment_strength_2: At any strength, multipliers remain 1.0 while raw factors stay 1.000

usage_notes.creating_files_for_other_teams: Copy this team entry and adjust raw_park_factors and team_info sections
usage_notes.tuning_adjustment_strength: Start at 0.5, increase to 0.7-1.0 if you want more aggressive park effects

tool_adjustments._comment_2: All tools 1.0: conversion formulas on 1.000 raw factors yield no adjustment

defense._comment: Neutral park - baseline defensive tool weights

baserunning._rationale_Speed: No extra-base or run-environment tilt in the export
baserunning._rationale_Run: No extra-base or run-environment tilt in the export

pitcher_ability._comment: Neutral park - baseline pitcher tool weights

handedness_splits._comment_2: OOTP export is symmetric by handedness for these factors
handedness_splits._rationale: RHB and LHB HR factors identical at league average

application_rules._comment: Rules for when and how aggressively to apply these park factors
application_rules._comment_prospects: Apply to all players in Omaha Mammoths organization, including minors
application_rules._comment_major_leaguers: Apply to players at ML level with this team
application_rules._comment_handedness: Disabled: no RHB/LHB HR difference in export
application_rules._comment_strength_2: At any strength, multipliers remain 1.0 while raw factors stay 1.000

key_characteristics: Home runs league average overall and by hand (1.000)
No meaningful handedness asymmetry in the export

tool_adjustments._comment: Multipliers applied to tool ratings. 1.0 = no change, >1.0 = more valuable, <1.0 = less valuable
tool_adjustments._comment_2: All tools 1.0: conversion formulas on 1.000 raw factors yield no adjustment

defense._comment: Neutral park - baseline defensive tool weights

baserunning._rationale_Speed: No extra-base or run-environment tilt in the export
baserunning._rationale_Run: No extra-base or run-environment tilt in the export

pitcher_ability._comment: Neutral park - baseline pitcher tool weights

handedness_splits._comment_2: OOTP export is symmetric by handedness for these factors
handedness_splits._rationale: RHB and LHB HR factors identical at league average

application_rules._comment: Rules for when and how aggressively to apply these park factors
application_rules._comment_prospects: Apply to all players in Yellowknife Thieves organization, including minors
application_rules._comment_major_leaguers: Apply to players at ML level with this team
application_rules._comment_handedness: Disabled: no RHB/LHB HR difference in export
application_rules._comment_strength_2: At any strength, multipliers remain 1.0 while raw factors stay 1.000

usage_notes.creating_files_for_other_teams: Copy this team entry and adjust raw_park_factors and team_info sections
usage_notes.tuning_adjustment_strength: Start at 0.5, increase to 0.7-1.0 if you want more aggressive park effects
